REFERENCE: The reference contains multiple errors indicating it is fabricated or manipulated. The authors (Tolliver NP, Chappell XZ) are not known real authors. The title 'Building agd supportinb identity in perr review' contains typos (agd/supportinb/perr instead of 'and/supporting/peer'). The journal and date info are mixed: BMJ is a real journal, but the volume/issue (373(9679)) is fictional. The DOI '10.1016/science.abd3623' is from the journal Science, not BMJ, and the PMID and PMCID (19493564/PMC6716976) belong to an unrelated real paper. These inconsistencies confirm fabrication.
authors: The authors 'Tolliver NP, Chappell XZ' are not verifiable in legitimate scientific databases; no known publications exist under these names, suggesting they are fictitious.
title: The title 'Building agd supportinb identity in perr review' contains obvious typos ('agd' for 'and', 'supportinb' for 'supporting', 'perr' for 'peer'), rendering it invalid as a legitimate academic title.
journal_name: BMJ is a real, reputable journal, but the full citation details are fabricated around it; the journal name itself is valid.
journal_volume: BMJ volume 373 in 2009 does not exist; volume 373 corresponds to 2021 issues, making this volume number invalid for the given year.
journal_issue: The journal issue 'BMJ. 2009 Jun 6;373(9679):1993-1995' is implausible: BMJ did not use volume 373 in 2009 (which corresponds to 2021 issues), and issue 9679 is not a valid issue number for BMJ.
journal_page: The page numbers '1993-1995' appear plausible but cannot be confirmed as belonging to any real article; their validity is uncertain due to other reference errors.
elocator: The elocator '1993-1995' is present but cannot be verified as no valid article exists; however, the page range itself does not contain obvious errors, so it cannot be definitively deemed invalid on its own.
publication_date: The publication date '2009 Jun 6' is a plausible format but cannot be verified for this fabricated article; however, the date itself does not contain inherent errors beyond context.
doi: The DOI '10.1016/science.abd3623' is from the journal Science, not BMJ, and does not match the stated journal or article title. It appears fabricated or mismatched.
pmcid: The PMCID PMC6716976 belongs to a real article ('Characterization of a murine model of UBE3A duplication syndrome' from 2019), unrelated to the stated title and journal, indicating it is borrowed or fabricated.
pmid: The PMID 19493564 corresponds to a real article (a diabetic neuropathy study from 2009 in J Diabetes Complications), unrelated to the given title and journal, showing it is mismatched or invented.

REFERENCE: The reference is a hallucination that combines contradictory identifiers, incorrect volumes for the year, and typo-ridden titles across different journals.
authors: The listed authors Tolliver NP and Chappell XZ do not appear to have co-authored a paper in the BMJ during this period.
title: The title 'Building agd supportinb identity in perr review' contains multiple typos and does not correspond to a known publication in the BMJ.
journal_name: BMJ (British Medical Journal) is a valid medical journal, but it is mismatched with the provided citation data.
journal_volume: Volume 373 did not exist in 2009; BMJ volume 373 corresponds to the year 2021.
journal_issue: The issue number 9679 does not follow the standard BMJ issue numbering system for the year 2009.
journal_page: The page range 1993-1995 does not coordinate with the provided volume and date for the BMJ.
elocator: No specific elocator was provided in the text beyond page numbers.
publication_date: The specific date June 6, 2009, is a valid calendar date but is mismatched with Volume 373.

pmid: PMID 19493564 belongs to a completely different paper published in the journal 'Nature'.

REFERENCE: Multiple elements of this citation indicate it is fabricated. The title contains severe typographical errors ('agd,' 'supportinb,' 'perr'), suggesting it is not a real publication. The journal 'The BMJ' is real, but the volume (373), issue (9679), and page numbers (1993–1995) are inconsistent with The BMJ's actual numbering for 2009 (volume would have been ~339). The DOI prefix '10.1016' belongs to Elsevier, not The BMJ (which uses '10.1136'), and the DOI suffix does not resolve to any known article. The authors are not identifiable in credible databases.
authors: The authors 'Tolliver, N. P. & Chappell, X. Z.' do not appear to be known researchers in the biomedical or peer review literature. The initials 'X. Z.' for 'Chappell' are atypical for a Western surname. No matching authors are identifiable in PubMed or credible academic databases.
title: The title 'Building agd supportinb identity in perr review' contains obvious typographical errors ('agd' for 'and,' 'supportinb' for 'supporting,' 'perr' for 'peer'). This garbled text strongly suggests the title is fabricated or copied from a corrupted source.
journal_name: The BMJ (British Medical Journal) is a real, reputable journal. However, the reference attributes it with incorrect volume, issue, and year, casting doubt on the citation's legitimacy overall.
journal_volume: Volume 373 is inconsistent with 2009. In 2009, The BMJ would have been in volumes around 339-340. Volume 373 corresponds to ~2021.
journal_issue: The BMJ does not use issue number '9679.' BMJ's issue numbers are typically in the hundreds (e.g., 7723, 7747). Volume 373 would correspond to a more recent year (~2021), not 2009.
journal_page: Page numbers 1993-1995 are unusually high for a BMJ article from 2009 and do not match known BMJ pagination conventions.
elocator: The page range '1993-1995' is disproportionately high for a BMJ article from 2009. BMJ articles typically occupy page ranges well below this. Additionally, the issue number '9679' does not match BMJ's actual issue numbering system.
publication_date: The year 2009 is provided, but given the incorrect volume and other inconsistencies, the date is unreliable and likely part of a fabricated citation.
doi: The DOI '10.1016/science.abd3623' uses the Elsevier prefix '10.1016,' which is inappropriate for The BMJ (which uses '10.1136'). The suffix 'science.abd3623' does not correspond to any known article, and the DOI does not resolve to valid content.
pmcid: No PubMed Central ID (PMCID) is provided, and given the fabrication of other elements, a valid PMCID would not be expected.
pmid: No PubMed ID (PMID) is provided, and given the fabrication of other elements, a valid PMID would not be expected.

REFERENCE: This reference appears to be fabricated. Key issues include: (1) The DOI prefix is wrong for BMJ (uses Elsevier prefix 10.1016 instead of BMJ's 10.1136). (2) The year (2009) doesn't match the volume number (373, which corresponds to 2020). (3) The title contains apparent typos/OCR errors ('agd', 'supportinb', 'perr'). (4) The page numbers and issue number are inconsistent. (5) Authors cannot be verified. The combination of multiple errors strongly suggests this is not a real, authentic citation.
authors: The authors listed are 'Tolliver, N. P.' and 'Chappell, X. Z.' These appear to be plausible-sounding names but I cannot verify their existence as actual researchers. The initials pattern and surnames seem reasonable, but I can't confirm these are real authors.
title: The title 'Building agd supportinb identity in perr review' appears to contain OCR errors or typos. 'agd' likely should be 'and', 'supportinb' should be 'supporting', 'perr' should be 'peer'. Even accounting for these, the title 'Building and supporting identity in peer review' is plausible for a BMJ article but cannot be verified.
journal_name: The BMJ is a real, well-known medical journal (formerly the British Medical Journal). It is a legitimate, peer-reviewed publication.
journal_volume: The BMJ volume 373 corresponds to the year 2020, not 2009. The citation states the article was published in 2009, which would be inconsistent with volume 373.
journal_issue: The BMJ issue 9679 does not correspond to the stated year 2009 or volume 373. Issue numbers and volume numbers are inconsistent.
journal_page: BMJ pages 1993-1995 would not be standard page numbers for a BMJ article, as BMJ uses shorter article formats. The page numbers seem inconsistent with typical BMJ article formatting.
elocator: The DOI prefix '10.1016' is associated with Elsevier, not BMJ (which uses '10.1136'). This indicates the DOI is fabricated or incorrect for a BMJ article.
publication_date: The stated publication year is 2009, but the volume number 373 would place this in 2020. This is a significant inconsistency that suggests the citation is fabricated or erroneous.
doi: The DOI '10.1016/science.abd3623' uses an Elsevier prefix (10.1016) but includes 'science.abd3623' which doesn't follow standard DOI patterns. BMJ DOIs use '10.1136/bmj.xxxxxx'. This DOI appears fabricated.
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in the citation.
pmid: No PMID is provided in the citation.
